key_insight: A machine-learning interatomic potential that splits crystal energy into intra- and intermolecular parts can approach DFT-level accuracy for crystal structure prediction while running much faster.
engineering_relevance: This enables larger CSP searches and more reliable ranking of candidate solid forms, especially when harmonic free energies are included for polymorph selection.
method: Use MACE-POLAR for intramolecular energies trained on OMol25.
Model intermolecular energies with MACE-POLAR + XDM-style dispersion + a learned delta correction to B86bPBE-XDM residuals.
Rank structures by energy and harmonic free energy to improve polymorph ordering.
evidence_signal: Validated on two independent evaluation sets (19 AstraZeneca compounds and 28 blind-test compounds), where performance was comparable to PBE+Neumann-Perrin and close to B86bPBE-XDM DFT, respectively.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on the training domain and harmonic approximation, so transfer to chemically different systems or strongly anharmonic crystals may require additional validation.
paper_title: DFT Accuracy on Crystal Structure Prediction with Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials
